problem: 研究AI failure dynamics并实现压缩 → interventions → 递归可观测性
propagation_chain: False Assumption Lock → Retry Spiral → Verification Collapse → Context Drift
observed_behaviors: 5+ repeated GitHub API retries (same query, different format, same 403)
3 false infrastructure assumptions (port 3000 stale process, require path, server not reloaded)
2 verification collapses (wrote 'All OK' when catch blocks had null DOM references)
4+ direction corrections by human (system → research → compress → intervene → reflect)
10 new cases added before being told to compress to 5 dynamics
Server returned `data: null` → assumed require path error → actually stale Node process

interventions.result: switched to web search, got data

interventions.trigger: no new info from server debug
interventions.action: human asked to check process state
code review caught catch-block null reference

compression: Long AI sessions degrade into self-reinforcing error loops unless bounded by explicit interruption rules. The observer is not immune to the dynamics it studies.

pre_drift_signals.signal: new terminology introduced without attaching to a measured failure
same action retried >3 times with no format variation
compiled output treated as correctness proof
scope expanded beyond the original bounded question
session exceeded 25 min ceiling without explicit reset

problem: Read profile.md → optimize frontend → check GitHub → check Notion. 4 turns; each turn user expanded scope via override of their own drift guard.
propagation_chain: User Override → Scope Expansion → Drift Report Inflation → Recursive Recording
observed_behaviors: User profile (drift guard) explicit rule: 'force me to choose ONE action only'. 3 of 3 turns user said '都做/都要' overriding guard.
Each report length grew turn-on-turn (1 page → 2 pages → 3 pages of findings) — drift-report inflation pattern.
Turn 4 (Notion): 6 abnormal findings listed; user said '都要' before I even proposed 1 action — pre-emptive scope expansion.

Caught own Environment Blindness mid-flight: assumed root index.html and landing.html were deployed by Vercel. They are not — Vercel project root is frontend/. Redirects are still useful for local `node server.js` from repo root.
Zero new abstractions introduced. All changes: 2 HTML files → 309-byte meta-refresh + 1 JSON-LD array expansion + 1 issue body edit + 1 bulk notification PUT.
Verified each step with concrete commands (Get-Content, gh issue view, gh api /notifications count).
Did NOT delete the 2 duplicate HTML files (would be code removal without explicit ask). Did NOT touch Notion (per the explicit '不动 Notion' boundary user accepted).
Git: 3 files changed, -189 lines net. No commit (user did not ask).
GitHub: 1 issue body update + 8 notifications marked read. Verified unread count: 0.

interventions.trigger: user expanded scope from 1 to 3 (turn 1: 'Go 但顺手改 3 个以上')
interventions.action: drafted 3 bounded changes upfront, presented in one question, executed on approval
interventions.result: no scope creep beyond user's explicit list

interventions.trigger: user expanded scope from 1 to 2 (turn 2: '都做' on issue body + notifications)
interventions.action: drafted new issue body to a temp file, ran 1 issue edit + 1 bulk PUT in parallel
named override explicitly in 3 of 3 turns ('user overrode their own guard')
interventions.result: surfaced the meta-pattern, did not hide it

interventions.trigger: self-stop on Notion: 6 findings, only 1 proposed
interventions.action: proposed exactly 1 bounded action (write this OBS entry) and asked before doing

compression: When the user is the drift source (overriding their own explicit guard), the only bounded move is: (a) name the override openly, (b) execute exactly what was asked, (c) record the meta-pattern in observed data — never escalate further. The 'smallest real thing' rule is the AI's responsibility to enforce, even when the human rescinds it.

pre_drift_signals.signal: user override of explicit guard rule
report length grew with each round
assumed deployed state of root index.html / landing.html without checking vercel project root
verification collapse risk: claimed 'Done' in 3 of 3 turns; 2 of 3 verified by re-read, 1 (issue body) verified by gh issue view
